prompt:
Our edge collectors ship telemetry blobs over links where we pay per byte, and the blobs are mostly repeated log lines. I benchmarked the usual suspects and the framing overhead plus cgo pain isn't worth it for our sizes — I want a tiny pure-Go codec we own, package lzrle at the root of module go-lzrle, stdlib only. I've written the wire format down properly because the decoder ships to collectors we can't redeploy quickly, so this is a contract, not a sketch.

Frame = 4-byte magic 'LZR1', 1 mode byte, big-endian uint32 of the ORIGINAL (uncompressed) length, then the body.

Mode 0 (raw): body is the original bytes verbatim. Mode 1 (compressed): body is a token stream over a 4096-byte sliding window, three token kinds: 0x01 n(u8) followed by n literal bytes; 0x02 n(u8) v(u8) meaning n copies of byte v; 0x03 d(u16 BE) L(u8) meaning copy L bytes starting d bytes back in the output produced so far, byte by byte, so d < L overlap is legal and repeats the pattern. Counts and lengths must be >= 1 on the wire; the encoder should only emit runs/matches of 3+ (shorter is a net loss) but the decoder accepts any >= 1.

API: Compress(data []byte) []byte, Decompress(frame []byte) ([]byte, error), plus sentinel errors ErrBadMagic, ErrTruncated, ErrCorrupt (errors.Is-matchable, wrapping fine).

Compress: byte-for-byte deterministic — same input, identical frame, every time; we dedupe uploads by hash. If the token stream comes out >= the original size, ship mode 0 instead (that also covers empty input). Genuinely incompressible data must therefore cost exactly 9 bytes of overhead, nothing more.

Decompress is the paranoid end, collectors feed it whatever the disk gives back: too short to even hold the header -> ErrTruncated; magic mismatch -> ErrBadMagic; unknown mode byte -> ErrCorrupt; raw body shorter than declared -> ErrTruncated, longer -> ErrCorrupt; token stream ending mid-token -> ErrTruncated; unknown tag, zero count/length, or a match whose distance is 0 or reaches back past the start of the output -> ErrCorrupt; and when the stream finishes, the output length must equal the declared length or it's ErrCorrupt — bail as soon as output would exceed it, don't let a corrupt run token allocate the moon.

lzrle_test.go is the acceptance contract (it hand-builds frames to hold the decoder to the spec above) — don't touch it. Done = go test -race -timeout 30s ./... green.
